layer_1_flower.intent: Happy tranquility in a tropical fruit garden

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Black Pepper carries the dominant beta-caryophyllene. Sage matches the ripe berries aroma note. Lemon provides Limonene support. Lemongrass rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A mid-energy session. Happy tranquility in a tropical fruit garden. Genres: indie rock, neo-soul, jazz, alternative. Tempo around 85-110 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: Madd Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Ripe Berries and papaya infused oolong blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 195°F.
Add oolong tea bag and steep for 3-4 minutes.
Add mixed berry compote and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add dried papaya and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: Madd Fizz
method: Fill a rocks glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz mixed berry shrub and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz fresh papaya juice.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with ripe berries.

cocktail.name: Madd Spritz
cocktail.description: Gin-based ripe berries and papaya cocktail
method: Combine 2 oz gin, 1.5 oz mixed berry shrub, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz fresh papaya juice.
Add ice.
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled highball.
Garnish with ripe berries.

juice.name: Madd Juice
juice.description: Cold-pressed mixed berries and papaya blend
method: Press 2 cups fresh mixed berries through a cold press.
Press 1 cup papaya through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: Madd Shot
ingredients: tiny pinch of hint of spice
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz gin with 0.75 oz fresh mixed berries juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Olive Tapenade Crostini
snack.desc: Kalamata olive tapenade on grilled sourdough with fresh basil and lemon zest.

appetizer.name: Charred Octopus with Chimichurri
appetizer.desc: Tender octopus charred over oak with fresh herb chimichurri and grilled lemon.

main.name: Wood-Fired Pizza with Fresh Herbs
main.desc: Sourdough crust with San Marzano tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, basil, and local olive oil.

dessert.name: Passion Fruit Mousse
dessert.desc: Light passion fruit mousse with toasted coconut and a lime tuile.

cheese.name: Humboldt Fog & Seasonal Fruit
cheese.desc: Cypress Grove Humboldt Fog with seasonal fruit, crackers, and honeycomb.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your happy space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Mixed seating heights, conversation-friendly arrangement. Ambient background music visible.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 25 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin with the first sip. Alternate breathing cycles with mindful tasting.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Find your center. Breathe in through the nose for four counts. Hold for four, noticing the stillness. Exhale slowly through the mouth for six counts, letting the breath carry tension away.

layer_13_narrative.text:
18 drops of black pepper carry the dominant β-Caryophyllene through the room steadily — the way Malama The Roots intended when they cultivated Madd Fruit in Potter Valley, Mendocino County. The lineage reads PapayaBerry Runtz X Moroccan Peaches, but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: ripe berries, papaya, and hint of spice.

The second oil — Sage — adds 8 drops of brightness that matches the papaya note in the flower itself. Relaxed is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body balances into happy with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 1.95% total can deliver.

In the warm gathering space, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of β-Caryophyllene, already primed for happy tranquility in a tropical fruit garden. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Malama The Roots grows in Potter Valley, Mendocino County: not just cannabis, but context. The hybrid genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as rosemary in a Mendocino garden. The session balances into its final form — tranquil, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at SF Botanical Garden — Fragrance Garden, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Rotundone in Syrah shares the spicy, peppery character of β-caryophyllene — same aromatic family, different kingdoms.
wine.tasting_note: Dark fruit, cracked pepper, coastal minerality

wine.terpene_bridge: Zinfandel's brambly spice and warm pepper notes echo caryophyllene's warming, anti-inflammatory character.

beer.terpene_bridge: Belgian yeast esters produce spicy, peppery phenolics that echo caryophyllene's warming quality.
Centennial hops carry significant caryophyllene — cannabis and hops are Cannabaceae cousins.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
